summary: Verstappen hunts redemption at Spa after back-to-back high-speed crashes force Red Bull to abandon their experimental rear wing and revert to conventional downforce. Norris's 10-place grid penalty hands the McLaren driver a mountain to climb on one of F1's fastest circuits, potentially reshuffling the podium equation in favor of front-runners not fighting through the field. A clean weekend for Verstappen and strong execution from Ferrari's rejuvenated lineup—buoyed by Hamilton's Spain victory—could decisively reshape the championship momentum heading into the season's second half.
